message:
test(scheduler): add audit regression tests for priority scheduler edge cases

Add five targeted regression tests covering scheduler invariants that
were identified during a systematic audit pass (asupersync-10x0x.78):

- schedule_timed_does_not_move_existing_ready_task: verifies that calling
  schedule_timed() on a task already in the ready lane is a no-op,
  preventing accidental demotion of ready work to the timed queue

- steal_ready_batch_maintains_scheduled_set_invariant: confirms that
  after stealing N tasks, the scheduler length decreases by exactly N,
  stolen tasks are no longer present in any lane, and remaining tasks
  still pop correctly without corruption

- dense_tag_at_max_generation_does_not_collide_with_sentinel: exercises
  the ScheduledSet with a TaskId at generation u32::MAX to verify that
  the computed dense tag (u32::MAX + 1 = 4294967296) never equals
  DENSE_COLLISION (u64::MAX), preventing phantom membership results

- move_to_cancel_lower_priority_is_noop: ensures that attempting to
  move a task to the cancel lane with a lower priority than its current
  cancel priority does not downgrade it, preserving priority ordering

- pop_timed_only_with_hint_groups_by_deadline_not_now: verifies that
  three tasks with the same deadline are all dispatchable via
  pop_timed_only_with_hint() with different RNG hints, and that the
  scheduler is empty afterward

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
